content.full_text:
7

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 6, 1992

Melba Bishop
Deputy Mayor
City of Oceanside
Civic Center
300 North Hill Street
Oceanside, CA 92054

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-92-356

Dear Ms. Bishop:

I am writing to confirm our telephone conversation of July 3,
1992, wherein we discussed your request for advice, letter No. A-
92-356. As the Deputy Mayor of the City of Oceanside, you are
requesting advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

You enclosed in your letter the June 10, 1992 memorandum
prepared by Mr. D. Dwight Worden, Special Counsel to the City of
Oceanside. Although Mr. Worden's memorandum to you discussed a
number of areas of law regarding conflicts of interest, we limited
our discussion to conflicts of interest under the Act.

From the facts presented in the memorandum, it appears that
Mr. John Patterson is not, and has not been, a source of income to
you within the last 12 months. If he is not a source of income to
you, there is not a conflict of interest under Section 87103(c).

The memorandum also stated that you do not have any business
or economic relations with Mr. Patterson and you do not have any
financial interest in any of the sites currently under
consideration for the Materials Recovery Facility.

_________________________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Our File No. A-92-356
Page 2


Therefore, I informed you that Mr. Worden's analysis under
the Act is accurate. However, should other facts arise, they must
also be evaluated in terms of a conflict of interest. I have
enclosed a pamphlet outlining conflicts of interest for public
officials.

I trust this answers your question.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Jill Stachel
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JS:aa

Enclosure

sections.question_synthetic: Does the Deputy Mayor of Oceanside have a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act regarding her relationship with John Patterson and potential involvement with a Materials Recovery Facility project?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: No conflict of interest exists under Section 87103(c) because John Patterson is not a source of income to the Deputy Mayor, and she has no business relations or financial interests in the sites under consideration for the Materials Recovery Facility.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; content extracted from body of advice letter. FPPC confirms prior analysis by city counsel. Conditional language regarding potential future facts noted.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does the Deputy Mayor of Oceanside have a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act regarding her relationship with John Patterson and potential involvement with a Materials Recovery Facility project?

CONCLUSION: No conflict of interest exists under Section 87103(c) because John Patterson is not a source of income to the Deputy Mayor, and she has no business relations or financial interests in the sites under consideration for the Materials Recovery Facility.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms advice to Deputy Mayor Melba Bishop of Oceanside regarding conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act, concluding that no conflict exists based on the facts presented regarding John Patterson and the Materials Recovery Facility project.
